questions.question: A cone has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 8 cm, what is its volume?

questions.question: True or False: The set of whole numbers \(W\) is a proper subset of the set of integers \(Z\).

questions.explanation: Even natural numbers start from 2 and increase by 2.

questions.question: True or False: The set of natural numbers is a proper subset of the set of whole numbers.
questions.explanation: Natural numbers (1,2,…) are contained in whole numbers (0,1,2,…), and not equal, so it is a proper subset.

questions.question: True or False: The set of all even integers is a subset of the set of natural numbers.
questions.explanation: Even integers include negative numbers, which are not natural numbers; therefore the statement is false.

questions.question: True or False: In a right‑angled triangle, the length of the altitude drawn from the right angle to the hypotenuse is always less than each of the two legs.
questions.explanation: True. The altitude to the hypotenuse is the geometric mean of the segments of the hypotenuse and is shorter than both legs.

questions.question: True or False: In a right‑angled triangle, the length of the altitude drawn from the right angle to the hypotenuse is always less than half the length of the hypotenuse.
questions.explanation: True. Using the geometric mean property, altitude = (product of the two segments of the hypotenuse)/hypotenuse, which is always less than half the hypotenuse.

questions.question: True or False: The set of all integers is a subset of the set of real numbers.
questions.explanation: Every integer is also a real number, so ℤ ⊂ ℝ.

questions.question: True or False: In a set of data, the mean is always equal to the median.
questions.explanation: False. Mean equals median only for symmetric distributions; generally they differ.

questions.question: True or False: The set of natural numbers \(N\) is a proper subset of the set of integers \(Z\).
questions.explanation: All natural numbers are integers, and \(N\) is contained in \(Z\) but not equal, so it is a proper subset.
